relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul refuses the idea that the Divine rejected his people.
Foreknown people are not abandoned.
Elijah’s loneliness becomes the scriptural appeal.
The answer reveals a hidden faithful remnant.
Grace keeps a remnant in the present.
Grace remains grace only when not turned into achievement.
Seeking, choosing, and hardening form Paul’s difficult tension.
Scripture names eyes and ears unable to receive.
David’s table image becomes a warning of entanglement.
Darkened eyes and bent backs name judgment imagery.
Stumbling is not final fall; nations’ saving/healing becomes provocation toward return.
If loss brings riches, fullness will be greater.
Paul speaks directly to the nations as their apostle.
His service seeks to awaken and save some of his own flesh.
Acceptance would be life from the dead.
Holy firstfruit and holy root carry the whole.
Wild olive branches share the rich root by grafting.
Do not boast over branches; the root supports you.
The grafted branch may misunderstand its place.
Standing is by trust, so pride must become reverence.
The warning applies to grafted branches too.
Kindness must be remained in, not possessed as superiority.
The Divine can graft again those who do not remain in untrust.
Natural branches belong to their own olive tree.
The mystery protects the nations from self-wisdom.
All Israel’s saving/healing is held in prophetic promise.
Covenant includes removal of misalignment.
Even in opposition, they remain beloved because of the fathers.
The gifts and calling of the Divine are irrevocable.
The nations know mercy through another’s disobedience.
Mercy shown to nations becomes mercy’s path back to Israel.
The Divine encloses all in disobedience so mercy may reach all.
Paul falls into depth: riches, wisdom, knowledge.
No one masters the mind of the Lord.
No one puts the Divine in debt.
From him, through him, and into him are all things.

contemplative_note: Romans 11 is the anti-boasting anchor. The grafted branch lives from the root; mercy is the final horizon, not replacement.
